State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901         322-6441

July 17, 1984

Stephen T. Terry
McDonald, Halsted & Laybourne
1200 Wilshire Blvd.
Los Angeles, CA  90017

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-84-164

Dear Mr. Terry:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you.  On June 13,
1984, you asked whether the term "employee," as used in 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18423, includes independent contractors.  This
regulation provides that, under certain circumstances, the
payment of salary, reimbursements for personal expenses, or
other compensation by an employer to an employee who spends more
than 10% of his compensated time in any one month rendering
services for political purposes is a contribution or an
expenditure.  I advised you that, in order to preserve the
purposes of the Political Reform Act's reporting provisions and
the regulation, the term "employee" must be interpreted to
include "independent contractors."

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

MACDONALD, HALSTED & LAYBOURNE
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
*PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

1200 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017

ALEXANDER MACDONALD
(1898-1970)

EVERET & LAYBOURNE
DAVID G. HALSTED
JOHN A. MACDONALD*
GILBERT C. CHAMP*
ORVILLE A. ARMSTRONG
FRANK D. MORRISON

THOMAS CURTIS, JR.*

JOHN A. SHINER
CYNTHIA HARDING RYAN*
ARTHUR MORRISON
BRIAN D. YOUNG*
THOMAS S. HOFER*
JOHN A. MOORE
GORDON E. BOSSERMAN
LAWRENCE D. STICKNEY
BRUCE E. DAVIES

JUN 18 12 12 PM '84

DAVID G. HALSTED
WILLIAM J. SHIRA
JEFFREY C. HEREIN
ALLEN F. COWAN
PAUL H. HAYABE
LEON S. BLUTERMAN
ANDREW R. DELPRETE
MARK D. SACHER
DARLENE E. PLAKE
BRUCE E. DAVIES
[illegible]
STEPHEN C. TERRY
MARK J. BRENWICK
MARY L. RUSSELL
DAVID H. THRELFALL
KEVIN T. WIPKING
CYNTHIA R. LIPNER

SAN DIEGO
WELLS FARGO BANK BUILDING
FOURTH & "F" STREETS
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(619) 231-1641

TELEPHONE: (213)-688-2111
TELECOPIER: (213)-481-8552
TELEX: 752716

TELEPHONE: (213) 481-17

*PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

E. STEVENS HALSTED, JR.
ROBERT M. EDWARDS
N. STEVEN CLINTON
COUNSEL

June 15, 1984

Barbara A. Millman, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Miss Millman:

    This letter is a follow-up to my June 13, 1984
telephone conversation with your office during which I re-
ceived an informal opinion concerning the definition of
"employee" as that term is used in California Administrative
Code §18423.

    Based upon this conversation it is my understand-
ing that both the Legal Department and the Technical Division
of the Fair Political Practices Commission would regard
consultants and independent contractors as "employees" for
the purpose of reporting payments for personal services as
contributions and expenditures under the Political Reform
Act of 1974.

    Since we represent business enterprises which
could incur reporting obligations under the Act, we would
like to obtain the Commission's official opinion concerning
the foregoing question.

    I do appreciate the courtesy extended to me in
this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Stephen T. Terry

STT:rh
